keyword.cluster: AI agent security enterprise OWASP top 10 agentic prompt injection NHI identity 2026
keyword.competition: medium-high — emerging category, not yet saturated
keyword.commercial_intent: very high — security budgets being allocated, vendor evaluation underway
keyword.trend_momentum: explosive. 97% of security leaders expect a material AI-agent-driven security incident in 2026. OWASP released first-ever Top 10 for Agentic Applications in Dec 2025 — ASI01-ASI10 now industry standard. Prompt injection attacks surged 340% in 2026. Non-human identities now outnumber human identities 144:1 in enterprises. Gartner flagged AI agent identity/governance as top cybersecurity trend for 2026. NHIs growing 44% yearly. Only 6% of security budgets allocated to agent security despite near-universal expectation of breach. Palo Alto Networks launched Prisma AIRS. Lasso Security, AGAT Software, and Obsidian Security all building dedicated agent security platforms.
secondary: agentic AI security framework best practices

selection_rationale.why_this_keyword: AI Agent Security is the inevitable next cluster after the lifecycle series (orchestration → governance → evaluation → deployment → frameworks). Every piece in that series touched on security concerns but none addressed them head-on. Security is the #1 blocker to production deployment — enterprises won't ship agents at scale until they can secure them. The OWASP ASI Top 10 (Dec 2025) gives the article a definitive framework to anchor on, and the 97% breach-expectation stat creates urgency. This is the article CISOs and security VPs are searching for right now.
timeliness_factors: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications released Dec 2025 — the first industry-standard taxonomy, now referenced by NIST, Palo Alto Networks, and enterprise security teams
97% of security leaders expect a major AI-agent-driven incident in 2026 (Security Boulevard, Apr 2026)
Prompt injection attacks surged 340% in 2026 (AI Magicx)
Non-human identities outnumber humans 144:1, growing 44% yearly (IDSA, 2026)
Gartner: 40% of enterprise apps will feature AI agents by end of 2026, up from <5% in 2025
Simon Willison's 'Lethal Trifecta' framework gaining traction — private data access + untrusted content exposure + exfiltration capability
Palo Alto Networks launched Prisma AIRS 2.0 for AI agent security
EchoLeak (CVE-2025-32711): CVSS 9.3 zero-click exploit against Microsoft 365 Copilot
Natural complement to prior series — governance (Run #2) covered control frameworks conceptually; this is the security implementation guide
selection_rationale.domain_alignment: Perfect — AgentForge builds and deploys AI agents. Security is the domain where knowledge becomes trust. A CISO evaluating whether to allow agentic AI in their enterprise is the highest-value reader we can acquire.
selection_rationale.differentiation_from_prior_runs: New cluster pivot after 5-part lifecycle series completion. Runs #1–5: how to build, govern, evaluate, deploy, and choose frameworks. Run #6: how to SECURE. This is the article that turns architecture readers into implementation readers — and security readers are the ones who hold budget authority.

research_sources.title: How to Secure Agentic AI in the Enterprise: Best Practices for 2026
AI Agent Security in 2026: What Enterprises Are Getting Wrong
Agentic AI Risks: OWASP Top 10 & Real-World Incidents
AI Agents Are Creating an Identity Security Crisis in 2026
Prompt Injection Attacks: The Hidden Security Crisis
OWASP Top 10 Agents & AI Vulnerabilities (2026 Cheat Sheet)
97% of Enterprises Expect Major AI Agent Security Incident

sections: Executive Summary / TL;DR — the 97% stat, OWASP ASI Top 10, and why this matters NOW
Why AI Agent Security Is Fundamentally Different (GEO definition block)
The OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications — Full Breakdown
The 5 Real-World Attacks That Changed Everything (EchoLeak, ForcedLeak, etc.)
The Non-Human Identity Crisis — 144:1 and Growing
The Execution Layer: Where Attacks Actually Happen
Prompt Injection: The #1 Threat and How to Defend
Runtime Monitoring: Why Logging Isn't Enough
Multi-Agent Security: Trust Boundaries Between Agents
Compliance & Governance: NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act, ISO 42001
Conclusion: Ship Secure or Don't Ship
article_plan.tone: Enterprise B2B security — authoritative, urgent but not alarmist, practical. Target Flesch 35+ with plain-language interludes. Title ≤60 chars. Meta description 150-160 chars with keyword + CTA. 10+ H2s, 2+ tables, 15+ hyperlinks.

geo_requirements.definition_block: 45-55 words in 'Why AI Agent Security Is Fundamentally Different' section
geo_requirements.faq_block: 5-6 questions with ## Frequently Asked Questions heading
geo_requirements.featured_image: Image marker required — Part B generates and inserts
